questions.question: Where must Sass variable overrides like `$brand-color: #2c5282;` be placed relative to `@import "minima";` for the theme to pick them up?
choices: Anywhere in the file - Sass resolves variables in any order
After the import, so they override the theme's defaults
Before the import, so the theme partial uses your values when it evaluates
Only inside an `@if` block guarded by `$override: true`
questions.explanation: Sass evaluates top-down. Variables must be assigned before `@import "minima";` so the theme's partial sees your value when it references the variable. Rules placed after the import are last-wins overrides for selectors instead.

questions.question: You want to change three lines in the theme's `<head>` and nothing else. Which workflow keeps your diff with upstream smallest?
choices: Fork the entire theme repo and edit `_includes/head.html` in the fork
Copy only `_includes/head.html` into your repo and edit the copy; leave every other theme file in the gem
Copy the whole `_includes/` directory into your repo so all includes are co-located
Edit `_includes/head.html` directly in the gem's install directory
questions.explanation: Jekyll's per-path precedence means you only need to shadow the one file. Copying the directory or forking the theme pulls files into your repo that you'll then have to merge on every upstream update.

questions.question: Why pin the theme's major version in `Gemfile` (e.g. `gem "minima", "~> 2.5"`) rather than tracking the latest release?
choices: Major version bumps can rename or remove files your overrides depend on; pinning makes the upgrade a deliberate decision
Pinning makes the build faster because Bundler skips dependency resolution
GitHub Pages refuses to build sites with unpinned theme gems
Sass cannot resolve variables across major versions of a theme
questions.explanation: Your override files target a specific theme structure. A major version may rename `_includes/head.html` or restructure Sass, silently breaking your overrides. Pinning forces you to upgrade intentionally and re-check the overrides.
